## Overview

**v0.5.2 — box-drawing and dim text rendered the way Windows Terminal does.**
A maintenance release focused on terminal-output fidelity: box-drawing
characters (the lines and borders that TUIs like Codex draw) now render as
continuous strokes instead of broken/dashed ones, and the SGR 2 *faint/dim*
attribute now actually dims text toward the background instead of being ignored.
Both were verified side-by-side with Windows Terminal on Linux, macOS, and
Windows. Plus a more grabbable scrollbar and a multi-line paste fix.

No config schema changes since v0.5.1. **macOS still requires macOS 14 (Sonoma)
or later.**

## What's new since v0.5.1

### Rendering

- **Box-drawing characters match Windows Terminal** ([#258](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/258))
  — U+2500–257F (straight lines, corners, T-junctions, crosses, double lines,
  dashed lines, rounded corners, diagonals) are now drawn procedurally instead
  of relying on font glyphs. Borders are continuous with no gaps, shading blocks
  (`░▒▓`) use smooth alpha instead of a dot dither, line thickness / dashed
  rhythm / rounded-corner radius follow Windows Terminal's `BuiltinGlyphs`
  formulas, and arcs and diagonals are anti-aliased. The same shared core is
  used by all three renderers (Direct3D 11, Metal, software).
- **Faint / dim text (SGR 2)** ([#258](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/258))
  — the faint attribute was previously ignored, so secondary UI text, borders,
  and inactive labels rendered at full brightness (looking white instead of
  gray). Faint text now blends toward the cell background (`fg*0.5 + bg*0.5`,
  matching Ghostty's default `faint-opacity` and Windows Terminal). Applied
  uniformly to regular text, block elements, and box-drawing on all three
  platforms.

### Scrollbar

- **Wider, easier-to-grab scrollbar** ([#259](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/259))
  — width grows 8 → 10 pt, and dragging the *body* of a long thumb (not just its
  edges) now scrolls. Unified across Linux, macOS, and Windows.

### Fixes

- **Box lines no longer vanish while idle — macOS** ([#261](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/261))
  — after the box-drawing rework, box lines on macOS only appeared while
  dragging and disappeared when rendering went idle. The instance buffer now
  grows dynamically so the lines stay drawn.
- **Multi-line paste newlines** ([#260](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/260))
  — multi-line paste now normalizes line breaks to CR consistently, regardless
  of platform.

### Build

- The ghostty dependency is pinned to `font-backend = .freetype`, which stops
  the libxml2 (via fontconfig) lazy dependency from being fetched. First builds
  on Windows now work without Developer Mode / symlink permission. CI uses the
  same path.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows 10/11 x64 | `tildaz-v0.5.2-win-x64.zip` | Unzip anywhere. `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY. Unsigned (same SmartScreen / AV handling as before). |
| Windows 11 ARM64 | `tildaz-v0.5.2-win-arm64.zip` | ARM64-native binary + ARM64 ConPTY. Unsigned. |
| macOS 14+ universal | `tildaz-v0.5.2-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel. Drag `TildaZ.app` to Applications. Ad-hoc signed. |
| Linux portable (glibc 2.28+) | `tildaz-v0.5.2-linux-{x86_64,aarch64}.tar.gz` | Extract, run `./install.sh` (writes `.desktop` + icon under `~/.local/share/`, no `sudo`). |
| Linux Debian / Ubuntu | `tildaz_0.5.2_{amd64,arm64}.deb` | `sudo dpkg -i tildaz_*.deb` or open with the Software app. |
| Linux Fedora / RHEL / openSUSE | `tildaz-0.5.2-1.{x86_64,aarch64}.rpm` | `sudo dnf install ./tildaz-*.rpm` |
| Linux distro-independent | `TildaZ-0.5.2-{x86_64,aarch64}.AppImage` | `chmod +x` and run. FUSE optional. glibc 2.28+. |
| Arch / Manjaro / EndeavourOS | `tildaz-0.5.2-1-x86_64.pkg.tar.zst` | `sudo pacman -U tildaz-*.pkg.tar.zst` (x86_64 only — Arch's official arch). |

**A running Wayland session (compositor) is required — TildaZ does not run
under X11.** It speaks the Wayland protocol directly over the socket, so it does
not link `libwayland-client`; the only build-time link is `glibc 2.28+`. The
`.deb` / `.rpm` declare `libxkbcommon`, `libfreetype`, `libfontconfig` as hard
dependencies; `libharfbuzz`, `libdbus-1`, and `libgio-2.0`, plus the
`xdg-desktop-portal` service, are recommended and loaded at runtime via `dlopen`,
degrading to a feature-off state (with a clear log line) when absent.

## Migration from v0.5.1

- **No config schema changes.** `~/.config/tildaz/config.json` (and the
  Windows/macOS equivalents) are read unchanged. The new faint-text dimming is
  fixed at the standard 0.5 opacity and is not configurable.
- **macOS minimum is still macOS 14 (Sonoma).** macOS 13 users should stay on
  v0.5.0.

## Known limitations

- **Text anti-aliasing mode differs from Windows Terminal** — TildaZ renders
  text with ClearType subpixel AA, while Windows Terminal's AtlasEngine defaults
  to grayscale AA. With a matching color scheme the difference is small, but on
  thin strokes it can shift the perceived color slightly. A grayscale AA option
  is under consideration as a follow-up.
- **macOS info / error dialog keys are intermittent** ([#249](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/249))
  — the NSAlert-based info / error dialog can occasionally ignore Esc / Enter
  because the accessory-app modal does not always become the key window.
- **KDE floating dock overlap** ([#206](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/206))
  — a Plasma *floating* panel can be partially covered (KDE Bug 491006).
  Workaround: turn off the panel's *Floating* option.
- **VSCode / Electron (X11) Hangul IME** ([#194](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/194))
  — an fcitx5 frontend-separation limitation, not fixable at the protocol level.
- The software `wl_shm` renderer is a placeholder until a GPU path lands.
